preview_text:
The model favors Australia at 44% to win, with Paraguay offered a 26% chance and a draw at 31%. This represents a competitive fixture where the underdog holds meaningful probability of victory rather than facing a steep climb.

The 31% draw probability suggests an evenly matched contest where stalemate ranks as a plausible outcome. The win probabilities show Australia with a narrow edge—roughly 18 percentage points clear of Paraguay—indicating the match is likely to be closely fought rather than one-sided. All three outcomes carry non-trivial weight in the model's distribution.

The model and sportsbooks diverge substantially. Books and Polymarket both favor Paraguay at roughly 44%, placing it as the consensus pick. The model's 26% Paraguay probability sits 18 percentage points below those prices, a gap that signals meaningful disagreement about relative strength. The model ranks Australia higher; market consensus ranks Paraguay higher. The dashboard offers no view on which assessment is correct.
